},
"computerUse":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ComputerUse"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Tool to support the model interacting directly with the\n      computer. If enabled, it automatically populates computer-use specific\n      Function Declarations."
},
"fileSearch":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/FileSearch"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Tool to retrieve knowledge from the File Search Stores."
},
"codeExecution":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ToolCodeExecution"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. CodeExecution tool type. Enables the model to execute code as part of generation."
},
"enterpriseWebSearch":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/EnterpriseWebSearch"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Tool to support searching public web data, powered by Vertex AI Search and Sec4 compliance. This field is not supported in Gemini API."
},
"googleMaps":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/GoogleMaps"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. GoogleMaps tool type. Tool to support Google Maps in Model."
},
"googleSearch":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/GoogleSearch"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. GoogleSearch tool type. Tool to support Google Search in Model. Powered by Google."
},
"urlContext":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/UrlContext"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Tool to support URL context retrieval."
}
},
"title":  "Tool",
"type":  "object"
},

"properties":  {},
"title":  "ToolCodeExecution",
"type":  "object"
},

},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

}
},
"title":  "ToolConfig",
"type":  "object"
},

],

"properties":  {},
"title":  "UrlContext",
"type":  "object"
},
"VertexAISearch":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "Retrieve from Vertex AI Search datastore or engine for grounding.\n\ndatastore and engine are mutually exclusive. See\nhttps://cloud.google.com/products/agent-builder. This data type is not\nsupported in Gemini API.",
"properties":  {
"dataStoreSpecs":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"$ref":  "#/$defs/VertexAISearchDataStoreSpec"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Specifications that define the specific DataStores to be searched, along with configurations for those data stores. This is only considered for Engines with multiple data stores. It should only be set if engine is used.",
"title":  "Datastorespecs"
},
"datastore":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Fully-qualified Vertex AI Search data store resource ID. Format: \`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/collections/{collection}/dataStores/{dataStore}\`",
"title":  "Datastore"
},
"engine":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Fully-qualified Vertex AI Search engine resource ID. Format: \`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/collections/{collection}/engines/{engine}\`",
"title":  "Engine"
},
"filter":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Filter strings to be passed to the search API.",
"title":  "Filter"
},
"maxResults":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Number of search results to return per query. The default value is 10. The maximumm allowed value is 10.",
"title":  "Maxresults"
}
},
"title":  "VertexAISearch",
"type":  "object"
},
"VertexAISearchDataStoreSpec":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "Define data stores within engine to filter on in a search call and configurations for those data stores.\n\nFor more information, see\nhttps://cloud.google.com/generative-ai-app-builder/docs/reference/rpc/google.cloud.discoveryengine.v1#datastorespec.\nThis data type is not supported in Gemini API.",
"properties":  {
"dataStore":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Full resource name of DataStore, such as Format: \`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/collections/{collection}/dataStores/{dataStore}\`",
"title":  "Datastore"
},
"filter":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Filter specification to filter documents in the data store specified by data\_store field. For more information on filtering, see \[Filtering]\(https://cloud.google.com/generative-ai-app-builder/docs/filter-search-metadata)",
"title":  "Filter"
}
},
"title":  "VertexAISearchDataStoreSpec",
"type":  "object"
},
"VertexRagStore":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "Retrieve from Vertex RAG Store for grounding.\n\nThis data type is not supported in Gemini API.",
"properties":  {
"ragCorpora":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"type":  "string"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Deprecated. Please use rag\_resources instead.",
"title":  "Ragcorpora"
},
"ragResources":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"$ref":  "#/$defs/VertexRagStoreRagResource"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. The representation of the rag source. It can be used to specify corpus only or ragfiles. Currently only support one corpus or multiple files from one corpus. In the future we may open up multiple corpora support.",
"title":  "Ragresources"
},
"ragRetrievalConfig":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/RagRetrievalConfig"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. The retrieval config for the Rag query."
},
"similarityTopK":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Number of top k results to return from the selected corpora.",
"title":  "Similaritytopk"
},
"storeContext":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Currently only supported for Gemini Multimodal Live API. In Gemini Multimodal Live API, if \`store\_context\` bool is specified, Gemini will leverage it to automatically memorize the interactions between the client and Gemini, and retrieve context when needed to augment the response generation for users' ongoing and future interactions.",
"title":  "Storecontext"
},
"vectorDistanceThreshold":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Only return results with vector distance smaller than the threshold.",
"title":  "Vectordistancethreshold"
}
},
"title":  "VertexRagStore",
"type":  "object"
},
"VertexRagStoreRagResource":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "The definition of the Rag resource.\n\nThis data type is not supported in Gemini API.",
"properties":  {
"ragCorpus":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. RagCorpora resource name. Format: \`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/ragCorpora/{rag\_corpus}\`",
"title":  "Ragcorpus"
},
"ragFileIds":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"type":  "string"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. rag\_file\_id. The files should be in the same rag\_corpus set in rag\_corpus field.",
"title":  "Ragfileids"
}
},
"title":  "VertexRagStoreRagResource",
"type":  "object"
},
"VideoMetadata":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "Metadata describes the input video content.",

"endOffset":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. The end offset of the video.",
"title":  "Endoffset"
},
"fps":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{

},

"title":  "Beta"
}
},

"ADAPTER\_SIZE\_FOUR",

],

*field* adapter\_size\_:  `Optional`\[[`AdapterSize`](#genai.types.AdapterSize "genai.types.AdapterSize")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'adapterSize')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.adapter_size "Link to this definition")

*field* batch\_size\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'batchSize')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.batch_size "Link to this definition")

The batch size hyperparameter for tuning. If not set, a default of 4 or 16 will be used based on the number of training examples.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* beta\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.beta "Link to this definition")

Weight for KL Divergence regularization, Preference Optimization tuning only.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

The description of the TuningJob

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* epoch\_count\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'epochCount')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.epoch_count "Link to this definition")

Number of complete passes the model makes over the entire training dataset during training.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* evaluation\_config\_:  `Optional`\[[`EvaluationConfig`](#genai.types.EvaluationConfig "genai.types.EvaluationConfig")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'evaluationConfig')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.evaluation_config "Link to this definition")

Evaluation config for the tuning job.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* export\_last\_checkpoint\_only\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'exportLastCheckpointOnly')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.export_last_checkpoint_only "Link to this definition")

If set to true, disable intermediate checkpoints and only the last checkpoint will be exported. Otherwise, enable intermediate checkpoints.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* http\_options\_:  `Optional`\[[`HttpOptions`](#genai.types.HttpOptions "genai.types.HttpOptions")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'httpOptions')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.http_options "Link to this definition")

Used to override HTTP request options.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* labels\_:  `Optional`\[`dict`\[`str`, `str`]]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.labels "Link to this definition")

Optional. The labels with user-defined metadata to organize TuningJob and generated resources such as Model and Endpoint. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See <https://goo.gl/xmQnxf> for more information and examples of labels.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* learning\_rate\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'learningRate')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.learning_rate "Link to this definition")

The learning rate hyperparameter for tuning. If not set, a default of 0.001 or 0.0002 will be calculated based on the number of training examples.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* learning\_rate\_multiplier\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'learningRateMultiplier')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.learning_rate_multiplier "Link to this definition")

Multiplier for adjusting the default learning rate.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* method\_:  `Optional`\[[`TuningMethod`](#genai.types.TuningMethod "genai.types.TuningMethod")]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.method "Link to this definition")

The method to use for tuning (SUPERVISED\_FINE\_TUNING or PREFERENCE\_TUNING). If not set, the default method (SFT) will be used.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* pre\_tuned\_model\_checkpoint\_id\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'preTunedModelCheckpointId')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.pre_tuned_model_checkpoint_id "Link to this definition")

The optional checkpoint id of the pre-tuned model to use for tuning, if applicable.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* tuned\_model\_display\_name\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'tunedModelDisplayName')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.tuned_model_display_name "Link to this definition")

The display name of the tuned Model. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* validation\_dataset\_:  `Optional`\[[`TuningValidationDataset`](#genai.types.TuningValidationDataset "genai.types.TuningValidationDataset")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'validationDataset')*[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobConfig.validation_dataset "Link to this definition")

Validation dataset for tuning. The dataset must be formatted as a JSONL file.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*pydantic model* genai.types.CreateTuningJobParameters[¶](#genai.types.CreateTuningJobParameters "Link to this definition")

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

"FunctionResponse":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "A function response.",
"properties":  {
"willContinue":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty \`response\` with \`will\_continue=False\` to signal that the function call is finished.",
"title":  "Willcontinue"
},

"type":  "null"
}
],

*class* genai.types.DatasetStatsDict[¶](#genai.types.DatasetStatsDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Statistics computed over a tuning dataset.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

total\_billable\_character\_count\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.DatasetStatsDict.total_billable_character_count "Link to this definition")

Output only. Number of billable characters in the tuning dataset.

total\_tuning\_character\_count\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.DatasetStatsDict.total_tuning_character_count "Link to this definition")

Output only. Number of tuning characters in the tuning dataset.

tuning\_dataset\_example\_count\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.DatasetStatsDict.tuning_dataset_example_count "Link to this definition")

Output only. Number of examples in the tuning dataset.

tuning\_step\_count\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.DatasetStatsDict.tuning_step_count "Link to this definition")

Output only. Number of tuning steps for this Tuning Job.

user\_dataset\_examples\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`ContentDict`](#genai.types.ContentDict "genai.types.ContentDict")]]\_[¶](#genai.types.DatasetStatsDict.user_dataset_examples "Link to this definition")

Output only. Sample user messages in the training dataset uri.

Bases: `BaseModel`

Optional parameters for models.get method.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

"FunctionResponse":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "A function response.",
"properties":  {
"willContinue":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty \`response\` with \`will\_continue=False\` to signal that the function call is finished.",
"title":  "Willcontinue"
},

Fields:

* [`training_dataset_stats (genai.types.DatasetStats | None)`](#genai.types.DistillationDataStats.training_dataset_stats "genai.types.DistillationDataStats.training_dataset_stats")

Validators:

*field* training\_dataset\_stats\_:  `Optional`\[[`DatasetStats`](#genai.types.DatasetStats "genai.types.DatasetStats")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'trainingDatasetStats')*[¶](#genai.types.DistillationDataStats.training_dataset_stats "Link to this definition")

Output only. Statistics computed for the training dataset.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*class* genai.types.DistillationDataStatsDict[¶](#genai.types.DistillationDataStatsDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Statistics computed for datasets used for distillation.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

training\_dataset\_stats\_:  `Optional`\[[`DatasetStatsDict`](#genai.types.DatasetStatsDict "genai.types.DatasetStatsDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.DistillationDataStatsDict.training_dataset_stats "Link to this definition")

Output only. Statistics computed for the training dataset.

*pydantic model* genai.types.Document[¶](#genai.types.Document "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

A Document is a collection of Chunks.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

"title":  "Name"
},

},
{

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*pydantic model* genai.types.DynamicRetrievalConfig[¶](#genai.types.DynamicRetrievalConfig "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Describes the options to customize dynamic retrieval.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "DynamicRetrievalConfig",
"description":  "Describes the options to customize dynamic retrieval.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"mode":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/DynamicRetrievalConfigMode"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The mode of the predictor to be used in dynamic retrieval."
},
"dynamicThreshold":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. The threshold to be used in dynamic retrieval. If not set, a system default value is used.",
"title":  "Dynamicthreshold"
}
},
"$defs":  {
"DynamicRetrievalConfigMode":  {
"description":  "Config for the dynamic retrieval config mode.",
"enum":  \[
"MODE\_UNSPECIFIED",
"MODE\_DYNAMIC"
],
"title":  "DynamicRetrievalConfigMode",
"type":  "string"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

Config for the dynamic retrieval config mode.

Run retrieval only when system decides it is necessary.

*pydantic model* genai.types.EditImageConfig[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfig "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Configuration for editing an image.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*class* genai.types.EditImageConfigDict[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Configuration for editing an image.

add\_watermark\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.add_watermark "Link to this definition")

Whether to add a watermark to the generated images.

aspect\_ratio\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.aspect_ratio "Link to this definition")

Aspect ratio of the generated images. Supported values are “1:1”, “3:4”, “4:3”, “9:16”, and “16:9”.

base\_steps\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.base_steps "Link to this definition")

The number of sampling steps. A higher value has better image quality, while a lower value has better latency.

edit\_mode\_:  `Optional`\[[`EditMode`](#genai.types.EditMode "genai.types.EditMode")]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.edit_mode "Link to this definition")

Describes the editing mode for the request.

guidance\_scale\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.guidance_scale "Link to this definition")

Controls how much the model adheres to the text prompt. Large values increase output and prompt alignment, but may compromise image quality.

http\_options\_:  `Optional`\[[`HttpOptionsDict`](#genai.types.HttpOptionsDict "genai.types.HttpOptionsDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.http_options "Link to this definition")

Used to override HTTP request options.

include\_rai\_reason\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.include_rai_reason "Link to this definition")

Whether to include the Responsible AI filter reason if the image is filtered out of the response.

include\_safety\_attributes\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.include_safety_attributes "Link to this definition")

Whether to report the safety scores of each generated image and the positive prompt in the response.

labels\_:  `Optional`\[`dict`\[`str`, `str`]]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.labels "Link to this definition")

User specified labels to track billing usage.

language\_:  `Optional`\[[`ImagePromptLanguage`](#genai.types.ImagePromptLanguage "genai.types.ImagePromptLanguage")]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.language "Link to this definition")

Language of the text in the prompt.

negative\_prompt\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.negative_prompt "Link to this definition")

Description of what to discourage in the generated images.

number\_of\_images\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.number_of_images "Link to this definition")

Number of images to generate.

output\_compression\_quality\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.output_compression_quality "Link to this definition")

Compression quality of the generated image (for `image/jpeg` only).

output\_gcs\_uri\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.output_gcs_uri "Link to this definition")

Cloud Storage URI used to store the generated images.

output\_mime\_type\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.output_mime_type "Link to this definition")

MIME type of the generated image.

person\_generation\_:  `Optional`\[[`PersonGeneration`](#genai.types.PersonGeneration "genai.types.PersonGeneration")]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.person_generation "Link to this definition")

Allows generation of people by the model.

safety\_filter\_level\_:  `Optional`\[[`SafetyFilterLevel`](#genai.types.SafetyFilterLevel "genai.types.SafetyFilterLevel")]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.safety_filter_level "Link to this definition")

Filter level for safety filtering.

seed\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.EditImageConfigDict.seed "Link to this definition")

Random seed for image generation. This is not available when `add_watermark` is set to true.

*pydantic model* genai.types.EditImageResponse[¶](#genai.types.EditImageResponse "Link to this definition")

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

"FunctionResponse":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "A function response.",
"properties":  {
"willContinue":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty \`response\` with \`will\_continue=False\` to signal that the function call is finished.",
"title":  "Willcontinue"
},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/FunctionResponseScheduling"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],

},

*pydantic model* genai.types.EmbedContentConfig[¶](#genai.types.EmbedContentConfig "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

*class* genai.types.EndSensitivity(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.EndSensitivity "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

End of speech sensitivity.

END\_SENSITIVITY\_HIGH *=  'END\_SENSITIVITY\_HIGH'*[¶](#genai.types.EndSensitivity.END_SENSITIVITY_HIGH "Link to this definition")

Automatic detection ends speech more often.

END\_SENSITIVITY\_LOW *=  'END\_SENSITIVITY\_LOW'*[¶](#genai.types.EndSensitivity.END_SENSITIVITY_LOW "Link to this definition")

Automatic detection ends speech less often.

END\_SENSITIVITY\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'END\_SENSITIVITY\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.EndSensitivity.END_SENSITIVITY_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

The default is END\_SENSITIVITY\_LOW.

"PhishBlockThreshold":  {
"description":  "Sites with confidence level chosen & above this value will be blocked from the search results.\n\nThis enum is not supported in Gemini API.",
"enum":  \[
"PHISH\_BLOCK\_THRESHOLD\_UNSPECIFIED",
"BLOCK\_LOW\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_MEDIUM\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_HIGH\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_HIGHER\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_VERY\_HIGH\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_ONLY\_EXTREMELY\_HIGH"
],

"type":  "string"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

Validators:

*field* blocking\_confidence\_:  `Optional`\[[`PhishBlockThreshold`](#genai.types.PhishBlockThreshold "genai.types.PhishBlockThreshold")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'blockingConfidence')*[¶](#genai.types.EnterpriseWebSearch.blocking_confidence "Link to this definition")

Optional. Sites with confidence level chosen & above this value will be blocked from the search results.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* exclude\_domains\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`str`]]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'excludeDomains')*[¶](#genai.types.EnterpriseWebSearch.exclude_domains "Link to this definition")

Optional. List of domains to be excluded from the search results. The default limit is 2000 domains.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*class* genai.types.EnterpriseWebSearchDict[¶](#genai.types.EnterpriseWebSearchDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Tool to search public web data, powered by Vertex AI Search and Sec4 compliance.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

blocking\_confidence\_:  `Optional`\[[`PhishBlockThreshold`](#genai.types.PhishBlockThreshold "genai.types.PhishBlockThreshold")]\_[¶](#genai.types.EnterpriseWebSearchDict.blocking_confidence "Link to this definition")

Optional. Sites with confidence level chosen & above this value will be blocked from the search results.

exclude\_domains\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`str`]]\_[¶](#genai.types.EnterpriseWebSearchDict.exclude_domains "Link to this definition")

Optional. List of domains to be excluded from the search results. The default limit is 2000 domains.

*pydantic model* genai.types.EntityLabel[¶](#genai.types.EntityLabel "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

An entity representing the segmented area.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*pydantic model* genai.types.EvaluationConfig[¶](#genai.types.EvaluationConfig "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Evaluation config for tuning.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*pydantic model* genai.types.ExecutableCode[¶](#genai.types.ExecutableCode "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Code generated by the model that is meant to be executed, and the result returned to the model.

Generated when using the \[CodeExecution] tool, in which the code will be automatically executed, and a corresponding \[CodeExecutionResult] will also be generated.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "ExecutableCode",
"description":  "Code generated by the model that is meant to be executed, and the result returned to the model.\n\nGenerated when using the \[CodeExecution] tool, in which the code will be\nautomatically executed, and a corresponding \[CodeExecutionResult] will also be\ngenerated.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"code":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Required. The code to be executed.",
"title":  "Code"
},
"language":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Language"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Required. Programming language of the \`code\`."
}
},
"$defs":  {
"Language":  {
"description":  "Programming language of the \`code\`.",
"enum":  \[
"LANGUAGE\_UNSPECIFIED",
"PYTHON"
],
"title":  "Language",
"type":  "string"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

*pydantic model* genai.types.ExternalApi[¶](#genai.types.ExternalApi "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Retrieve from data source powered by external API for grounding.

The external API is not owned by Google, but need to follow the pre-defined API spec. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "ExternalApi",
"description":  "Retrieve from data source powered by external API for grounding.\n\nThe external API is not owned by Google, but need to follow the pre-defined\nAPI spec. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.",

"description":  "The service account used to generate an OpenID Connect (OIDC)-compatible JWT token signed by the Google OIDC Provider (accounts.google.com) for extension endpoint (https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/create-short-lived-credentials-direct#sa-credentials-oidc). - The audience for the token will be set to the URL in the server url defined in the OpenApi spec. - If the service account is provided, the service account should grant \`iam.serviceAccounts.getOpenIdToken\` permission to Vertex AI Extension Service Agent (https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/access-control#service-agents).",

}

*class* genai.types.ExternalApiDict[¶](#genai.types.ExternalApiDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Retrieve from data source powered by external API for grounding.

The external API is not owned by Google, but need to follow the pre-defined API spec. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

*class* genai.types.FileDict[¶](#genai.types.FileDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

A file uploaded to the API.

create\_time\_:  `Optional`\[`datetime`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.create_time "Link to this definition")

Output only. The timestamp of when the File was created.

display\_name\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.display_name "Link to this definition")

‘Welcome Image’

Type:

Optional. The human-readable display name for the File. The display name must be no more than 512 characters in length, including spaces. Example

download\_uri\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.download_uri "Link to this definition")

Output only. The URI of the File, only set for downloadable (generated) files.

error\_:  `Optional`\[[`FileStatusDict`](#genai.types.FileStatusDict "genai.types.FileStatusDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.error "Link to this definition")

Output only. Error status if File processing failed.

expiration\_time\_:  `Optional`\[`datetime`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.expiration_time "Link to this definition")

Output only. The timestamp of when the File will be deleted. Only set if the File is scheduled to expire.

mime\_type\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.mime_type "Link to this definition")

Output only. MIME type of the file.

name\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.name "Link to this definition")

files/123-456

Type:

The File resource name. The ID (name excluding the “files/” prefix) can contain up to 40 characters that are lowercase alphanumeric or dashes (-). The ID cannot start or end with a dash. If the name is empty on create, a unique name will be generated. Example

sha256\_hash\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.sha256_hash "Link to this definition")

Output only. SHA-256 hash of the uploaded bytes. The hash value is encoded in base64 format.

size\_bytes\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.size_bytes "Link to this definition")

Output only. Size of the file in bytes.

source\_:  `Optional`\[[`FileSource`](#genai.types.FileSource "genai.types.FileSource")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.source "Link to this definition")

Output only. The source of the File.

state\_:  `Optional`\[[`FileState`](#genai.types.FileState "genai.types.FileState")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.state "Link to this definition")

Output only. Processing state of the File.

update\_time\_:  `Optional`\[`datetime`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.update_time "Link to this definition")

Output only. The timestamp of when the File was last updated.

uri\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.uri "Link to this definition")

Output only. The URI of the File.

video\_metadata\_:  `Optional`\[`dict`\[`str`, `Any`]]\_[¶](#genai.types.FileDict.video_metadata "Link to this definition")

Output only. Metadata for a video.

*class* genai.types.FinishReason(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

Output only. The reason why the model stopped generating tokens.

If empty, the model has not stopped generating the tokens.

BLOCKLIST *=  'BLOCKLIST'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.BLOCKLIST "Link to this definition")

Token generation stopped because the content contains forbidden terms.

FINISH\_REASON\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'FINISH\_REASON\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.FINISH_REASON_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

The finish reason is unspecified.

IMAGE\_OTHER *=  'IMAGE\_OTHER'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.IMAGE_OTHER "Link to this definition")

Image generation stopped for a reason not otherwise specified.

IMAGE\_PROHIBITED\_CONTENT *=  'IMAGE\_PROHIBITED\_CONTENT'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.IMAGE_PROHIBITED_CONTENT "Link to this definition")

Image generation stopped because the generated images have prohibited content.

IMAGE\_RECITATION *=  'IMAGE\_RECITATION'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.IMAGE_RECITATION "Link to this definition")

Image generation stopped because the generated image may be a recitation from a source.

IMAGE\_SAFETY *=  'IMAGE\_SAFETY'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.IMAGE_SAFETY "Link to this definition")

Token generation stopped because generated images have safety violations.

LANGUAGE *=  'LANGUAGE'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.LANGUAGE "Link to this definition")

The token generation stopped because of using an unsupported language.

MALFORMED\_FUNCTION\_CALL *=  'MALFORMED\_FUNCTION\_CALL'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL "Link to this definition")

The function call generated by the model is invalid.

MAX\_TOKENS *=  'MAX\_TOKENS'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.MAX_TOKENS "Link to this definition")

Token generation reached the configured maximum output tokens.

NO\_IMAGE *=  'NO\_IMAGE'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.NO_IMAGE "Link to this definition")

The model was expected to generate an image, but none was generated.

OTHER *=  'OTHER'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.OTHER "Link to this definition")

All other reasons that stopped the token generation.

PROHIBITED\_CONTENT *=  'PROHIBITED\_CONTENT'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.PROHIBITED_CONTENT "Link to this definition")

Token generation stopped for potentially containing prohibited content.

RECITATION *=  'RECITATION'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.RECITATION "Link to this definition")

The token generation stopped because of potential recitation.

SAFETY *=  'SAFETY'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.SAFETY "Link to this definition")

When streaming, \[content]\[] is empty if content filters blocks the output.

Type:

Token generation stopped because the content potentially contains safety violations. NOTE

SPII *=  'SPII'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.SPII "Link to this definition")

Token generation stopped because the content potentially contains Sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (SPII).

STOP *=  'STOP'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.STOP "Link to this definition")

Token generation reached a natural stopping point or a configured stop sequence.

UNEXPECTED\_TOOL\_CALL *=  'UNEXPECTED\_TOOL\_CALL'*[¶](#genai.types.FinishReason.UNEXPECTED_TOOL_CALL "Link to this definition")

The tool call generated by the model is invalid.

*pydantic model* genai.types.FunctionCall[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCall "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

A function call.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*pydantic model* genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Function calling config.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "FunctionCallingConfig",
"description":  "Function calling config.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"mode":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/FunctionCallingConfigMode"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Function calling mode."
},
"allowedFunctionNames":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"type":  "string"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Function names to call. Only set when the Mode is ANY. Function names should match \[FunctionDeclaration.name]. With mode set to ANY, model will predict a function call from the set of function names provided.",
"title":  "Allowedfunctionnames"
},
"streamFunctionCallArguments":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. When set to true, arguments of a single function call will be streamed out in multiple parts/contents/responses. Partial parameter results will be returned in the \[FunctionCall.partial\_args] field. This field is not supported in Gemini API.",
"title":  "Streamfunctioncallarguments"
}
},
"$defs":  {
"FunctionCallingConfigMode":  {
"description":  "Config for the function calling config mode.",
"enum":  \[
"MODE\_UNSPECIFIED",
"AUTO",
"ANY",
"NONE",
"VALIDATED"
],
"title":  "FunctionCallingConfigMode",
"type":  "string"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

Fields:

* [`allowed_function_names (list[str] | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.allowed_function_names "genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.allowed_function_names")

* [`mode (genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.mode "genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.mode")

* [`stream_function_call_arguments (bool | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.stream_function_call_arguments "genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.stream_function_call_arguments")

Validators:

*field* allowed\_function\_names\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`str`]]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'allowedFunctionNames')*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.allowed_function_names "Link to this definition")

Optional. Function names to call. Only set when the Mode is ANY. Function names should match \[FunctionDeclaration.name]. With mode set to ANY, model will predict a function call from the set of function names provided.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* mode\_:  `Optional`\[[`FunctionCallingConfigMode`](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode "genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode")]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.mode "Link to this definition")

Optional. Function calling mode.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* stream\_function\_call\_arguments\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'streamFunctionCallArguments')*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfig.stream_function_call_arguments "Link to this definition")

Optional. When set to true, arguments of a single function call will be streamed out in multiple parts/contents/responses. Partial parameter results will be returned in the \[FunctionCall.partial\_args] field. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*class* genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigDict[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Function calling config.

allowed\_function\_names\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`str`]]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigDict.allowed_function_names "Link to this definition")

Optional. Function names to call. Only set when the Mode is ANY. Function names should match \[FunctionDeclaration.name]. With mode set to ANY, model will predict a function call from the set of function names provided.

mode\_:  `Optional`\[[`FunctionCallingConfigMode`](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode "genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigDict.mode "Link to this definition")

Optional. Function calling mode.

stream\_function\_call\_arguments\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigDict.stream_function_call_arguments "Link to this definition")

Optional. When set to true, arguments of a single function call will be streamed out in multiple parts/contents/responses. Partial parameter results will be returned in the \[FunctionCall.partial\_args] field. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

*class* genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

Config for the function calling config mode.

ANY *=  'ANY'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.ANY "Link to this definition")

Model is constrained to always predicting function calls only. If “allowed\_function\_names” are set, the predicted function calls will be limited to any one of “allowed\_function\_names”, else the predicted function calls will be any one of the provided “function\_declarations”.

AUTO *=  'AUTO'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.AUTO "Link to this definition")

Default model behavior, model decides to predict either function calls or natural language response.

MODE\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'MODE\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.MODE_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

The function calling config mode is unspecified. Should not be used.

NONE *=  'NONE'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.NONE "Link to this definition")

Model will not predict any function calls. Model behavior is same as when not passing any function declarations.

VALIDATED *=  'VALIDATED'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.VALIDATED "Link to this definition")

Model decides to predict either a function call or a natural language response, but will validate function calls with constrained decoding. If “allowed\_function\_names” are set, the predicted function call will be limited to any one of “allowed\_function\_names”, else the predicted function call will be any one of the provided “function\_declarations”.

*pydantic model* genai.types.FunctionDeclaration[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Defines a function that the model can generate JSON inputs for.

The inputs are based on [OpenAPI 3.0 specifications](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3).

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "FunctionDeclaration",
"description":  "Defines a function that the model can generate JSON inputs for.\n\nThe inputs are based on \`OpenAPI 3.0 specifications\n\<https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3>\`\_.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"behavior":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Behavior"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Defines the function behavior."
},
"description":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Description and purpose of the function. Model uses it to decide how and whether to call the function.",
"title":  "Description"
},
"name":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Required. The name of the function to call. Must start with a letter or an underscore. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores, dots and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.",
"title":  "Name"
},
"parameters":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Schema"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Describes the parameters to this function in JSON Schema Object format. Reflects the Open API 3.03 Parameter Object. string Key: the name of the parameter. Parameter names are case sensitive. Schema Value: the Schema defining the type used for the parameter. For function with no parameters, this can be left unset. Parameter names must start with a letter or an underscore and must only contain chars a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or underscores with a maximum length of 64. Example with 1 required and 1 optional parameter: type: OBJECT properties: param1: type: STRING param2: type: INTEGER required: - param1"
},
"parametersJsonSchema":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Describes the parameters to the function in JSON Schema format. The schema must describe an object where the properties are the parameters to the function. For example: `{ \\"type\\": \\"object\\", \\"properties\\": { \\"name\\": { \\"type\\": \\"string\\" }, \\"age\\": { \\"type\\": \\"integer\\" } }, \\"additionalProperties\\": false, \\"required\\": \[\\"name\\", \\"age\\"\], \\"propertyOrdering\\": \[\\"name\\", \\"age\\"\] }` This field is mutually exclusive with \`parameters\`.",
"title":  "Parametersjsonschema"
},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Schema"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. Reflects the Open API 3.03 Response Object. The Schema defines the type used for the response value of the function."
},
"responseJsonSchema":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. The value specified by the schema is the response value of the function. This field is mutually exclusive with \`response\`.",
"title":  "Responsejsonschema"
}
},
"$defs":  {

"UNSPECIFIED",

],

},

{},

],

For example, the following schema defines a reference to a schema node named \\"Pet\\": type: object properties: pet: ref: #/defs/Pet defs: Pet: type: object properties: name: type: string The value of the \\"pet\\" property is a reference to the schema node named \\"Pet\\". * [`behavior (genai.types.Behavior | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.behavior "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.behavior")

* [`description (str | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.description "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.description")

* [`name (str | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.name "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.name")

* [`parameters (genai.types.Schema | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.parameters "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.parameters")

* [`parameters_json_schema (Any | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.parameters_json_schema "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.parameters_json_schema")

* [`response (genai.types.Schema | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.response "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.response")

* [`response_json_schema (Any | None)`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.response_json_schema "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.response_json_schema")

Validators:

*field* behavior\_:  `Optional`\[[`Behavior`](#genai.types.Behavior "genai.types.Behavior")]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.behavior "Link to this definition")

Defines the function behavior.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* description\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.description "Link to this definition")

Optional. Description and purpose of the function. Model uses it to decide how and whether to call the function.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* name\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.name "Link to this definition")

Required. The name of the function to call. Must start with a letter or an underscore. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores, dots and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* parameters\_:  `Optional`\[[`Schema`](#genai.types.Schema "genai.types.Schema")]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.parameters "Link to this definition")

Optional. Describes the parameters to this function in JSON Schema Object format. Reflects the Open API 3.03 Parameter Object. string Key: the name of the parameter. Parameter names are case sensitive. Schema Value: the Schema defining the type used for the parameter. For function with no parameters, this can be left unset. Parameter names must start with a letter or an underscore and must only contain chars a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or underscores with a maximum length of 64. Example with 1 required and 1 optional parameter: type: OBJECT properties: param1: type: STRING param2: type: INTEGER required: - param1

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* parameters\_json\_schema\_:  `Optional`\[`Any`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'parametersJsonSchema')*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.parameters_json_schema "Link to this definition")

Optional. Describes the parameters to the function in JSON Schema format. The schema must describe an object where the properties are the parameters to the function. For example: `` ` { "type": "object", "properties": { "name": { "type": "string" }, "age": { "type": "integer" } }, "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["name", "age"], "propertyOrdering": ["name", "age"] } ` `` This field is mutually exclusive with parameters.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* response\_:  `Optional`\[[`Schema`](#genai.types.Schema "genai.types.Schema")]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.response "Link to this definition")

Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. Reflects the Open API 3.03 Response Object. The Schema defines the type used for the response value of the function.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* response\_json\_schema\_:  `Optional`\[`Any`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'responseJsonSchema')*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.response_json_schema "Link to this definition")

Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. The value specified by the schema is the response value of the function. This field is mutually exclusive with response.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*classmethod* from\_callable(*\**, *client*, *callable*, *behavior=None*)[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.from_callable "Link to this definition")

Converts a Callable to a FunctionDeclaration based on the client.

Note: For best results prefer \[Google-style docstring]\(<https://google.github.io/styleguide/pyguide.html#383-functions-and-methods>) when describing arguments. This function does **not** parse argument descriptions into the property description slots of the resulting structure. Instead it sends the whole docstring in the top-level function description. Google-style docstring are closest to what the model is trained on.

Return type:

[`FunctionDeclaration`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration")

*classmethod* from\_callable\_with\_api\_option(*\**, *callable*, *api\_option='GEMINI\_API'*, *behavior=None*)[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration.from_callable_with_api_option "Link to this definition")

Converts a Callable to a FunctionDeclaration based on the API option.

Supported API option is ‘VERTEX\_AI’ or ‘GEMINI\_API’. If api\_option is unset, it will default to ‘GEMINI\_API’. If unsupported api\_option is provided, it will raise ValueError.

Return type:

[`FunctionDeclaration`](#genai.types.FunctionDeclaration "genai.types.FunctionDeclaration")

*class* genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Defines a function that the model can generate JSON inputs for.

The inputs are based on [OpenAPI 3.0 specifications](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3).

behavior\_:  `Optional`\[[`Behavior`](#genai.types.Behavior "genai.types.Behavior")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict.behavior "Link to this definition")

Defines the function behavior.

description\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict.description "Link to this definition")

Optional. Description and purpose of the function. Model uses it to decide how and whether to call the function.

name\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict.name "Link to this definition")

Required. The name of the function to call. Must start with a letter or an underscore. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores, dots and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

parameters\_:  `Optional`\[[`SchemaDict`](#genai.types.SchemaDict "genai.types.SchemaDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict.parameters "Link to this definition")

the Schema defining the type used for the parameter. For function with no parameters, this can be left unset. Parameter names must start with a letter or an underscore and must only contain chars a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or underscores with a maximum length of 64. Example with 1 required and 1 optional parameter: type: OBJECT properties: param1: type: STRING param2: type: INTEGER required: - param1

Type:

Optional. Describes the parameters to this function in JSON Schema Object format. Reflects the Open API 3.03 Parameter Object. string Key

Type:

the name of the parameter. Parameter names are case sensitive. Schema Value

parameters\_json\_schema\_:  `Optional`\[`Any`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict.parameters_json_schema "Link to this definition")

`` ` { "type": "object", "properties": { "name": { "type": "string" }, "age": { "type": "integer" } }, "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["name", "age"], "propertyOrdering": ["name", "age"] } ` `` This field is mutually exclusive with parameters.

Type:

Optional. Describes the parameters to the function in JSON Schema format. The schema must describe an object where the properties are the parameters to the function. For example

response\_:  `Optional`\[[`SchemaDict`](#genai.types.SchemaDict "genai.types.SchemaDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict.response "Link to this definition")

Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. Reflects the Open API 3.03 Response Object. The Schema defines the type used for the response value of the function.

response\_json\_schema\_:  `Optional`\[`Any`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionDeclarationDict.response_json_schema "Link to this definition")

Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. The value specified by the schema is the response value of the function. This field is mutually exclusive with response.

*pydantic model* genai.types.FunctionResponse[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponse "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

A function response.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Validators:

*field* id\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponse.id "Link to this definition")

Optional. The id of the function call this response is for. Populated by the client to match the corresponding function call id.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* name\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponse.name "Link to this definition")

Required. The name of the function to call. Matches \[FunctionDeclaration.name] and \[FunctionCall.name].

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* parts\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`FunctionResponsePart`](#genai.types.FunctionResponsePart "genai.types.FunctionResponsePart")]]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponse.parts "Link to this definition")

List of parts that constitute a function response. Each part may have a different IANA MIME type.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* response\_:  `Optional`\[`dict`\[`str`, `Any`]]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponse.response "Link to this definition")

Required. The function response in JSON object format. Use “output” key to specify function output and “error” key to specify error details (if any). If “output” and “error” keys are not specified, then whole “response” is treated as function output.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* scheduling\_:  `Optional`\[[`FunctionResponseScheduling`](#genai.types.FunctionResponseScheduling "genai.types.FunctionResponseScheduling")]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponse.scheduling "Link to this definition")

Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation. Only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. Defaults to WHEN\_IDLE.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* will\_continue\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'willContinue')*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponse.will_continue "Link to this definition")

Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty response with will\_continue=False to signal that the function call is finished.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation. Only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. Defaults to WHEN\_IDLE.

will\_continue\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponseDict.will_continue "Link to this definition")

Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty response with will\_continue=False to signal that the function call is finished.

*pydantic model* genai.types.FunctionResponsePart[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponsePart "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

A datatype containing media that is part of a FunctionResponse message.

A FunctionResponsePart consists of data which has an associated datatype. A FunctionResponsePart can only contain one of the accepted types in FunctionResponsePart.data.

A FunctionResponsePart must have a fixed IANA MIME type identifying the type and subtype of the media if the inline\_data field is filled with raw bytes.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*class* genai.types.FunctionResponsePartDict[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponsePartDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

A datatype containing media that is part of a FunctionResponse message.

A FunctionResponsePart consists of data which has an associated datatype. A FunctionResponsePart can only contain one of the accepted types in FunctionResponsePart.data.

A FunctionResponsePart must have a fixed IANA MIME type identifying the type and subtype of the media if the inline\_data field is filled with raw bytes.

file\_data\_:  `Optional`\[[`FunctionResponseFileDataDict`](#genai.types.FunctionResponseFileDataDict "genai.types.FunctionResponseFileDataDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponsePartDict.file_data "Link to this definition")

Optional. URI based data.

inline\_data\_:  `Optional`\[[`FunctionResponseBlobDict`](#genai.types.FunctionResponseBlobDict "genai.types.FunctionResponseBlobDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponsePartDict.inline_data "Link to this definition")

Optional. Inline media bytes.

*class* genai.types.FunctionResponseScheduling(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponseScheduling "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation.

INTERRUPT *=  'INTERRUPT'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponseScheduling.INTERRUPT "Link to this definition")

Add the result to the conversation context, interrupt ongoing generation and prompt to generate output.

SCHEDULING\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'SCHEDULING\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponseScheduling.SCHEDULING_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

This value is unused.

SILENT *=  'SILENT'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponseScheduling.SILENT "Link to this definition")

Only add the result to the conversation context, do not interrupt or trigger generation.

WHEN\_IDLE *=  'WHEN\_IDLE'*[¶](#genai.types.FunctionResponseScheduling.WHEN_IDLE "Link to this definition")

Add the result to the conversation context, and prompt to generate output without interrupting ongoing generation.

Validators:

*field* completions\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`GeminiPreferenceExampleCompletion`](#genai.types.GeminiPreferenceExampleCompletion "genai.types.GeminiPreferenceExampleCompletion")]]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.GeminiPreferenceExample.completions "Link to this definition")

List of completions for a given prompt.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* contents\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`Content`](#genai.types.Content "genai.types.Content")]]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.GeminiPreferenceExample.contents "Link to this definition")

Multi-turn contents that represents the Prompt.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*pydantic model* genai.types.GeminiPreferenceExampleCompletion[¶](#genai.types.GeminiPreferenceExampleCompletion "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Completion and its preference score.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

List of completions for a given prompt.

Multi-turn contents that represents the Prompt.

*pydantic model* genai.types.GenerateContentConfig[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentConfig "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Optional model configuration parameters.

For more information, see [Content generation parameters](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/multimodal/content-generation-parameters).

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "GenerateContentConfig",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"httpOptions":  {
"default":  null,

},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},
"systemInstruction":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Content"
},
{
"type":  "string"
},
{

},

}
]
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Instructions for the model to steer it toward better performance.\n      For example, \\"Answer as concisely as possible\\" or \\"Don't use technical\n      terms in your response\\".\n      ",
"title":  "Systeminstruction"
},
"temperature":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Value that controls the degree of randomness in token selection.\n      Lower temperatures are good for prompts that require a less open-ended or\n      creative response, while higher temperatures can lead to more diverse or\n      creative results.\n      ",
"title":  "Temperature"
},
"topP":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Tokens are selected from the most to least probable until the sum\n      of their probabilities equals this value. Use a lower value for less\n      random responses and a higher value for more random responses.\n      ",
"title":  "Topp"
},
"topK":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "For each token selection step, the \`\`top\_k\`\` tokens with the\n      highest probabilities are sampled. Then tokens are further filtered based\n      on \`\`top\_p\`\` with the final token selected using temperature sampling. Use\n      a lower number for less random responses and a higher number for more\n      random responses.\n      ",
"title":  "Topk"
},
"candidateCount":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Number of response variations to return.\n      ",
"title":  "Candidatecount"
},
"maxOutputTokens":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the response.\n      ",
"title":  "Maxoutputtokens"
},
"stopSequences":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"type":  "string"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],

"anyOf":  \[
{

"title":  "Logprobs"
},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}

"title":  "Frequencypenalty"
},
"seed":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "When \`\`seed\`\` is fixed to a specific number, the model makes a best\n      effort to provide the same response for repeated requests. By default, a\n      random number is used.\n      ",
"title":  "Seed"
},
"responseMimeType":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output response mimetype of the generated candidate text.\n      Supported mimetype:\n        - \`text/plain\`: (default) Text output.\n        - \`application/json\`: JSON response in the candidates.\n      The model needs to be prompted to output the appropriate response type,\n      otherwise the behavior is undefined.\n      This is a preview feature.\n      ",
"title":  "Responsemimetype"
},
"responseSchema":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"additionalProperties":  true,
"type":  "object"
},
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Schema"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The \`Schema\` object allows the definition of input and output data types.\n      These types can be objects, but also primitives and arrays.\n      Represents a select subset of an \[OpenAPI 3.0 schema\n      object]\(https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#schema).\n      If set, a compatible response\_mime\_type must also be set.\n      Compatible mimetypes: \`application/json\`: Schema for JSON response.\n\n      If \`response\_schema\` doesn't process your schema correctly, try using\n      \`response\_json\_schema\` instead.\n      ",
"title":  "Responseschema"
},
"responseJsonSchema":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Output schema of the generated response.\n      This is an alternative to \`response\_schema\` that accepts \[JSON\n      Schema]\(https://json-schema.org/). If set, \`response\_schema\` must be\n      omitted, but \`response\_mime\_type\` is required. While the full JSON Schema\n      may be sent, not all features are supported. Specifically, only the\n      following properties are supported: - `$id` - `$defs` - `$ref` - `$anchor`\n      - \`type\` - \`format\` - \`title\` - \`description\` - \`enum\` (for strings and\n      numbers) - \`items\` - \`prefixItems\` - \`minItems\` - \`maxItems\` - \`minimum\` -\n      \`maximum\` - \`anyOf\` - \`oneOf\` (interpreted the same as \`anyOf\`) -\n      \`properties\` - \`additionalProperties\` - \`required\` The non-standard\n      \`propertyOrdering\` property may also be set. Cyclic references are\n      unrolled to a limited degree and, as such, may only be used within\n      non-required properties. (Nullable properties are not sufficient.) If\n      `$ref` is set on a sub-schema, no other properties, except for than those\n      starting as a `$`, may be set.",
