],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Confidence score of the support references. Ranges from 0 to 1. 1 is the most confident. For Gemini 2.0 and before, this list must have the same size as the grounding\_chunk\_indices. For Gemini 2.5 and after, this list will be empty and should be ignored.",
"title":  "Confidencescores"
},
"groundingChunkIndices":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"type":  "integer"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "A list of indices (into 'grounding\_chunk') specifying the citations associated with the claim. For instance \[1,3,4] means that grounding\_chunk\[1], grounding\_chunk\[3], grounding\_chunk\[4] are the retrieved content attributed to the claim.",
"title":  "Groundingchunkindices"
},
"segment":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Segment"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Segment of the content this support belongs to."
}
},
"title":  "GroundingSupport",
"type":  "object"
},

},

"type":  "null"

"description":  "Optional. Score indicating how likely information from Google Search could help answer the prompt. The score is in the range `\[0, 1\]`, where 0 is the least likely and 1 is the most likely. This score is only populated when Google Search grounding and dynamic retrieval is enabled. It will be compared to the threshold to determine whether to trigger Google Search.",

}
},

*property* code\_execution\_result\_:  str  |  None\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentResponse.code_execution_result "Link to this definition")

Returns the code execution result in the response.

If there are multiple candidates, this returns the code execution result from only the first one.

*property* executable\_code\_:  str  |  None\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentResponse.executable_code "Link to this definition")

Returns the executable code in the response.

If there are multiple candidates, this returns the executable code from only the first one.

*property* function\_calls\_:  list\[[FunctionCall](#genai.types.FunctionCall "genai.types.FunctionCall")]  |  None\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentResponse.function_calls "Link to this definition")

Returns the list of function calls in the response.

If there are multiple candidates, this returns the function calls from only the first one.

*property* parts\_:  list\[[Part](#genai.types.Part "genai.types.Part")]  |  None\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentResponse.parts "Link to this definition")

Returns the content-parts in the response.

If there are multiple candidates, returns the parts from only the first one.

*property* text\_:  str  |  None\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentResponse.text "Link to this definition")

Returns the concatenation of all text parts in the response.

If there are multiple candidates, returns the text from only the first one. If there are non-text parts in the response, this returns only the text parts.

*pydantic model* genai.types.GenerateContentResponsePromptFeedback[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentResponsePromptFeedback "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Content filter results for a prompt sent in the request.

Note: This is sent only in the first stream chunk and only if no candidates were generated due to content violations.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

"title":  "GenerateContentResponsePromptFeedback",
"description":  "Content filter results for a prompt sent in the request.\n\nNote: This is sent only in the first stream chunk and only if no candidates\nwere generated due to content violations.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"blockReason":  {
"anyOf":  \[

"$ref":  "#/$defs/BlockedReason"
},
{

}

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{

}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. A readable message that explains the reason why the prompt was blocked. This field is not supported in Gemini API.",

"type":  "null"

],
"title":  "HarmBlockThreshold",

},

"description":  "Harm category.",
"enum":  \[

"HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_DANGEROUS\_CONTENT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_HARASSMENT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_SEXUALLY\_EXPLICIT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_JAILBREAK"
],
"title":  "HarmCategory",

},

"description":  "Output only. Harm probability levels in the content.",
"enum":  \[
"HARM\_PROBABILITY\_UNSPECIFIED",
"NEGLIGIBLE",
"LOW",

"HIGH"
],
"title":  "HarmProbability",
"type":  "string"
},
"HarmSeverity":  {
"description":  "Output only.\n\nHarm severity levels in the content. This enum is not supported in Gemini API.",

],

},

"anyOf":  \[
{

},
{

}
],

},

{

*pydantic model* genai.types.GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Usage metadata about the content generation request and response.

This message provides a detailed breakdown of token usage and other relevant metrics. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata",
"description":  "Usage metadata about the content generation request and response.\n\nThis message provides a detailed breakdown of token usage and other relevant\nmetrics. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"cacheTokensDetails":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ModalityTokenCount"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the cached content.",
"title":  "Cachetokensdetails"
},
"cachedContentTokenCount":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. The number of tokens in the cached content that was used for this request.",
"title":  "Cachedcontenttokencount"
},
"candidatesTokenCount":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The total number of tokens in the generated candidates.",
"title":  "Candidatestokencount"
},
"candidatesTokensDetails":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ModalityTokenCount"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the generated candidates.",
"title":  "Candidatestokensdetails"
},
"promptTokenCount":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The total number of tokens in the prompt. This includes any text, images, or other media provided in the request. When \`cached\_content\` is set, this also includes the number of tokens in the cached content.",
"title":  "Prompttokencount"
},
"promptTokensDetails":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ModalityTokenCount"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the prompt.",
"title":  "Prompttokensdetails"
},
"thoughtsTokenCount":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. The number of tokens that were part of the model's generated \\"thoughts\\" output, if applicable.",
"title":  "Thoughtstokencount"
},
"toolUsePromptTokenCount":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. The number of tokens in the results from tool executions, which are provided back to the model as input, if applicable.",
"title":  "Tooluseprompttokencount"
},
"toolUsePromptTokensDetails":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ModalityTokenCount"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

"title":  "Tooluseprompttokensdetails"
},
"totalTokenCount":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

"title":  "Totaltokencount"
},
"trafficType":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/TrafficType"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. The traffic type for this request."
}
},

"MediaModality":  {

{

}

}

*class* genai.types.GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadataDict[¶](#genai.types.GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadataDict "Link to this definition")

This message provides a detailed breakdown of token usage and other relevant metrics. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

*pydantic model* genai.types.GenerateImagesConfig[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfig "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

The config for generating an images.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*class* genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

The config for generating an images.

add\_watermark\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.add_watermark "Link to this definition")

Whether to add a watermark to the generated images.

aspect\_ratio\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.aspect_ratio "Link to this definition")

Aspect ratio of the generated images. Supported values are “1:1”, “3:4”, “4:3”, “9:16”, and “16:9”.

enhance\_prompt\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.enhance_prompt "Link to this definition")

Whether to use the prompt rewriting logic.

guidance\_scale\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.guidance_scale "Link to this definition")

Controls how much the model adheres to the text prompt. Large values increase output and prompt alignment, but may compromise image quality.

http\_options\_:  `Optional`\[[`HttpOptionsDict`](#genai.types.HttpOptionsDict "genai.types.HttpOptionsDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.http_options "Link to this definition")

Used to override HTTP request options.

image\_size\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.image_size "Link to this definition")

The size of the largest dimension of the generated image. Supported sizes are 1K and 2K (not supported for Imagen 3 models).

include\_rai\_reason\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.include_rai_reason "Link to this definition")

Whether to include the Responsible AI filter reason if the image is filtered out of the response.

include\_safety\_attributes\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.include_safety_attributes "Link to this definition")

Whether to report the safety scores of each generated image and the positive prompt in the response.

labels\_:  `Optional`\[`dict`\[`str`, `str`]]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.labels "Link to this definition")

User specified labels to track billing usage.

Language of the text in the prompt.

negative\_prompt\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.negative_prompt "Link to this definition")

Description of what to discourage in the generated images.

number\_of\_images\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.number_of_images "Link to this definition")

Number of images to generate.

output\_compression\_quality\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.output_compression_quality "Link to this definition")

Compression quality of the generated image (for `image/jpeg` only).

output\_gcs\_uri\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.output_gcs_uri "Link to this definition")

Cloud Storage URI used to store the generated images.

output\_mime\_type\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateImagesConfigDict.output_mime_type "Link to this definition")

MIME type of the generated image.

Filter level for safety filtering.

Random seed for image generation. This is not available when `add_watermark` is set to true.

*pydantic model* genai.types.GenerateVideosConfig[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfig "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Configuration for generating videos.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*field* person\_generation\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'personGeneration')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfig.person_generation "Link to this definition")

Whether allow to generate person videos, and restrict to specific ages. Supported values are: dont\_allow, allow\_adult.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* pubsub\_topic\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'pubsubTopic')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfig.pubsub_topic "Link to this definition")

The pubsub topic where to publish the video generation progress.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* reference\_images\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`VideoGenerationReferenceImage`](#genai.types.VideoGenerationReferenceImage "genai.types.VideoGenerationReferenceImage")]]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'referenceImages')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfig.reference_images "Link to this definition")

The images to use as the references to generate the videos. If this field is provided, the text prompt field must also be provided. The image, video, or last\_frame field are not supported. Each image must be associated with a type. Veo 2 supports up to 3 asset images *or* 1 style image.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* resolution\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfig.resolution "Link to this definition")

The resolution for the generated video. 720p and 1080p are supported.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* seed\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfig.seed "Link to this definition")

The RNG seed. If RNG seed is exactly same for each request with unchanged inputs, the prediction results will be consistent. Otherwise, a random RNG seed will be used each time to produce a different result.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

compression\_quality\_:  `Optional`\[[`VideoCompressionQuality`](#genai.types.VideoCompressionQuality "genai.types.VideoCompressionQuality")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.compression_quality "Link to this definition")

Compression quality of the generated videos.

duration\_seconds\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.duration_seconds "Link to this definition")

Duration of the clip for video generation in seconds.

enhance\_prompt\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.enhance_prompt "Link to this definition")

Whether to use the prompt rewriting logic.

fps\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.fps "Link to this definition")

Frames per second for video generation.

generate\_audio\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.generate_audio "Link to this definition")

Whether to generate audio along with the video.

http\_options\_:  `Optional`\[[`HttpOptionsDict`](#genai.types.HttpOptionsDict "genai.types.HttpOptionsDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.http_options "Link to this definition")

Used to override HTTP request options.

last\_frame\_:  `Optional`\[[`ImageDict`](#genai.types.ImageDict "genai.types.ImageDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.last_frame "Link to this definition")

Image to use as the last frame of generated videos. Only supported for image to video use cases.

mask\_:  `Optional`\[[`VideoGenerationMaskDict`](#genai.types.VideoGenerationMaskDict "genai.types.VideoGenerationMaskDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.mask "Link to this definition")

The mask to use for generating videos.

negative\_prompt\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.negative_prompt "Link to this definition")

Explicitly state what should not be included in the generated videos.

number\_of\_videos\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.number_of_videos "Link to this definition")

Number of output videos.

output\_gcs\_uri\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.output_gcs_uri "Link to this definition")

The gcs bucket where to save the generated videos.

person\_generation\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.person_generation "Link to this definition")

Whether allow to generate person videos, and restrict to specific ages. Supported values are: dont\_allow, allow\_adult.

pubsub\_topic\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.pubsub_topic "Link to this definition")

The pubsub topic where to publish the video generation progress.

reference\_images\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`VideoGenerationReferenceImageDict`](#genai.types.VideoGenerationReferenceImageDict "genai.types.VideoGenerationReferenceImageDict")]]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.reference_images "Link to this definition")

The images to use as the references to generate the videos. If this field is provided, the text prompt field must also be provided. The image, video, or last\_frame field are not supported. Each image must be associated with a type. Veo 2 supports up to 3 asset images *or* 1 style image.

resolution\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.resolution "Link to this definition")

The resolution for the generated video. 720p and 1080p are supported.

seed\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerateVideosConfigDict.seed "Link to this definition")

The RNG seed. If RNG seed is exactly same for each request with unchanged inputs, the prediction results will be consistent. Otherwise, a random RNG seed will be used each time to produce a different result.

Validators:

*field* audio\_timestamp\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'audioTimestamp')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.audio_timestamp "Link to this definition")

Optional. If enabled, audio timestamp will be included in the request to the model. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* candidate\_count\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'candidateCount')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.candidate_count "Link to this definition")

Optional. Number of candidates to generate.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* enable\_affective\_dialog\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'enableAffectiveDialog')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.enable_affective_dialog "Link to this definition")

Optional. If enabled, the model will detect emotions and adapt its responses accordingly. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* enable\_enhanced\_civic\_answers\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'enableEnhancedCivicAnswers')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.enable_enhanced_civic_answers "Link to this definition")

Optional. Enables enhanced civic answers. It may not be available for all models. This field is not supported in Vertex AI.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* frequency\_penalty\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'frequencyPenalty')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.frequency_penalty "Link to this definition")

Optional. Frequency penalties.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* logprobs\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.logprobs "Link to this definition")

Optional. Logit probabilities.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* max\_output\_tokens\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'maxOutputTokens')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.max_output_tokens "Link to this definition")

Optional. The maximum number of output tokens to generate per message.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* media\_resolution\_:  `Optional`\[[`MediaResolution`](#genai.types.MediaResolution "genai.types.MediaResolution")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'mediaResolution')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.media_resolution "Link to this definition")

Optional. If specified, the media resolution specified will be used.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* model\_selection\_config\_:  `Optional`\[[`ModelSelectionConfig`](#genai.types.ModelSelectionConfig "genai.types.ModelSelectionConfig")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'modelSelectionConfig')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.model_selection_config "Link to this definition")

Optional. Config for model selection.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* presence\_penalty\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'presencePenalty')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.presence_penalty "Link to this definition")

Optional. Positive penalties.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* response\_json\_schema\_:  `Optional`\[`Any`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'responseJsonSchema')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.response_json_schema "Link to this definition")

Output schema of the generated response. This is an alternative to response\_schema that accepts \[JSON Schema]\(<https://json-schema.org/>).

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* response\_logprobs\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'responseLogprobs')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.response_logprobs "Link to this definition")

Optional. If true, export the logprobs results in response.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* response\_mime\_type\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'responseMimeType')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.response_mime_type "Link to this definition")

Optional. Output response mimetype of the generated candidate text. Supported mimetype: - text/plain: (default) Text output. - application/json: JSON response in the candidates. The model needs to be prompted to output the appropriate response type, otherwise the behavior is undefined. This is a preview feature.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* response\_modalities\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`Modality`](#genai.types.Modality "genai.types.Modality")]]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'responseModalities')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.response_modalities "Link to this definition")

Optional. The modalities of the response.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* response\_schema\_:  `Optional`\[[`Schema`](#genai.types.Schema "genai.types.Schema")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'responseSchema')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.response_schema "Link to this definition")

Optional. The Schema object allows the definition of input and output data types. These types can be objects, but also primitives and arrays. Represents a select subset of an \[OpenAPI 3.0 schema object]\(<https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#schema>). If set, a compatible response\_mime\_type must also be set. Compatible mimetypes: application/json: Schema for JSON response.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* routing\_config\_:  `Optional`\[[`GenerationConfigRoutingConfig`](#genai.types.GenerationConfigRoutingConfig "genai.types.GenerationConfigRoutingConfig")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'routingConfig')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.routing_config "Link to this definition")

Optional. Routing configuration. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* seed\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.seed "Link to this definition")

Optional. Seed.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* speech\_config\_:  `Optional`\[[`SpeechConfig`](#genai.types.SpeechConfig "genai.types.SpeechConfig")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'speechConfig')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.speech_config "Link to this definition")

Optional. The speech generation config.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* stop\_sequences\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`str`]]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'stopSequences')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.stop_sequences "Link to this definition")

Optional. Stop sequences.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* temperature\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.temperature "Link to this definition")

Optional. Controls the randomness of predictions.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* thinking\_config\_:  `Optional`\[[`ThinkingConfig`](#genai.types.ThinkingConfig "genai.types.ThinkingConfig")]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'thinkingConfig')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.thinking_config "Link to this definition")

Optional. Config for thinking features. An error will be returned if this field is set for models that don’t support thinking.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* top\_k\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'topK')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.top_k "Link to this definition")

Optional. If specified, top-k sampling will be used.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* top\_p\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'topP')*[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfig.top_p "Link to this definition")

Optional. If specified, nucleus sampling will be used.

*class* genai.types.GenerationConfigDict[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Generation config.

audio\_timestamp\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.audio_timestamp "Link to this definition")

Optional. If enabled, audio timestamp will be included in the request to the model. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

candidate\_count\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.candidate_count "Link to this definition")

Optional. Number of candidates to generate.

enable\_affective\_dialog\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.enable_affective_dialog "Link to this definition")

Optional. If enabled, the model will detect emotions and adapt its responses accordingly. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

enable\_enhanced\_civic\_answers\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.enable_enhanced_civic_answers "Link to this definition")

Optional. Enables enhanced civic answers. It may not be available for all models. This field is not supported in Vertex AI.

frequency\_penalty\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.frequency_penalty "Link to this definition")

Optional. Frequency penalties.

logprobs\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.logprobs "Link to this definition")

Optional. Logit probabilities.

max\_output\_tokens\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.max_output_tokens "Link to this definition")

Optional. The maximum number of output tokens to generate per message.

media\_resolution\_:  `Optional`\[[`MediaResolution`](#genai.types.MediaResolution "genai.types.MediaResolution")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.media_resolution "Link to this definition")

Optional. If specified, the media resolution specified will be used.

model\_selection\_config\_:  `Optional`\[[`ModelSelectionConfigDict`](#genai.types.ModelSelectionConfigDict "genai.types.ModelSelectionConfigDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.model_selection_config "Link to this definition")

Optional. Config for model selection.

presence\_penalty\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.presence_penalty "Link to this definition")

Optional. Positive penalties.

response\_json\_schema\_:  `Optional`\[`Any`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.response_json_schema "Link to this definition")

Output schema of the generated response. This is an alternative to response\_schema that accepts \[JSON Schema]\(<https://json-schema.org/>).

response\_logprobs\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.response_logprobs "Link to this definition")

Optional. If true, export the logprobs results in response.

response\_mime\_type\_:  `Optional`\[`str`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.response_mime_type "Link to this definition")

(default) Text output. - application/json: JSON response in the candidates. The model needs to be prompted to output the appropriate response type, otherwise the behavior is undefined. This is a preview feature.

Type:

Optional. Output response mimetype of the generated candidate text. Supported mimetype

Type:

* text/plain

temperature\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.temperature "Link to this definition")

Optional. Controls the randomness of predictions.

thinking\_config\_:  `Optional`\[[`ThinkingConfigDict`](#genai.types.ThinkingConfigDict "genai.types.ThinkingConfigDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.thinking_config "Link to this definition")

Optional. Config for thinking features. An error will be returned if this field is set for models that don’t support thinking.

top\_k\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.top_k "Link to this definition")

Optional. If specified, top-k sampling will be used.

top\_p\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GenerationConfigDict.top_p "Link to this definition")

Optional. If specified, nucleus sampling will be used.

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},

"anyOf":  \[

"$ref":  "#/$defs/ThinkingLevel"
},
{

}
],

}
},

*pydantic model* genai.types.GoogleRpcStatus[¶](#genai.types.GoogleRpcStatus "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

The Status type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs.

It is used by \[gRPC]\(<https://github.com/grpc>). Each Status message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the \[API Design Guide]\(<https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors>). This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "GoogleRpcStatus",
"description":  "The \`Status\` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs.\n\nIt is used by \[gRPC]\(https://github.com/grpc). Each \`Status\` message contains\nthree pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can\nfind out more about this error model and how to work with it in the \[API\nDesign Guide]\(https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors). This data type is\nnot supported in Gemini API.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"code":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.",
"title":  "Code"
},
"details":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"additionalProperties":  true,
"type":  "object"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.",
"title":  "Details"
},
"message":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.",
"title":  "Message"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

"title":  "GoogleSearch",

"properties":  {

"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"type":  "string"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. List of domains to be excluded from the search results. The default limit is 2000 domains. Example: \[\\"amazon.com\\", \\"facebook.com\\"]. This field is not supported in Gemini API.",
"title":  "Excludedomains"
},
"blockingConfidence":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/PhishBlockThreshold"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Sites with confidence level chosen & above this value will be blocked from the search results. This field is not supported in Gemini API."
},
"timeRangeFilter":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Interval"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Filter search results to a specific time range. If customers set a start time, they must set an end time (and vice versa). This field is not supported in Vertex AI."
}
},
"$defs":  {
"Interval":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a Timestamp end (exclusive).\n\nThe start must be less than or equal to the end. When the start equals the\nend, the interval is empty (matches no time). When both start and end are\nunspecified, the interval matches any time.",
"properties":  {
"endTime":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"format":  "date-time",
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Exclusive end of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the end.",
"title":  "Endtime"
},
"startTime":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"format":  "date-time",
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Inclusive start of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same or after the start.",
"title":  "Starttime"
}
},
"title":  "Interval",
"type":  "object"
},
"PhishBlockThreshold":  {
"description":  "Sites with confidence level chosen & above this value will be blocked from the search results.\n\nThis enum is not supported in Gemini API.",
"enum":  \[
"PHISH\_BLOCK\_THRESHOLD\_UNSPECIFIED",
"BLOCK\_LOW\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_MEDIUM\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_HIGH\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_HIGHER\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_VERY\_HIGH\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_ONLY\_EXTREMELY\_HIGH"
],
"title":  "PhishBlockThreshold",
"type":  "string"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

*pydantic model* genai.types.GoogleTypeDate[¶](#genai.types.GoogleTypeDate "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday.

The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following: \* A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values. \* A month and day, with a zero year (for example, an anniversary). \* A year on its own, with a zero month and a zero day. \* A year and month, with a zero day (for example, a credit card expiration date). Related types: \* google.type.TimeOfDay \* google.type.DateTime \* google.protobuf.Timestamp. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "GoogleTypeDate",
"description":  "Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday.\n\nThe time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are\ninsignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can\nrepresent one of the following: \* A full date, with non-zero year, month, and\nday values. \* A month and day, with a zero year (for example, an anniversary).\n\* A year on its own, with a zero month and a zero day. \* A year and month,\nwith a zero day (for example, a credit card expiration date). Related types: \*\ngoogle.type.TimeOfDay \* google.type.DateTime \* google.protobuf.Timestamp. This\ndata type is not supported in Gemini API.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"day":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0 to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn't significant.",
"title":  "Day"
},
"month":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a month and day.",
"title":  "Month"
},
"year":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without a year.",
"title":  "Year"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

*class* genai.types.GoogleTypeDateDict[¶](#genai.types.GoogleTypeDateDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday.

The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following: \* A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values. \* A month and day, with a zero year (for example, an anniversary). \* A year on its own, with a zero month and a zero day. \* A year and month, with a zero day (for example, a credit card expiration date). Related types: \* google.type.TimeOfDay \* google.type.DateTime \* google.protobuf.Timestamp. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

day\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GoogleTypeDateDict.day "Link to this definition")

Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0 to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn’t significant.

month\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GoogleTypeDateDict.month "Link to this definition")

Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a month and day.

year\_:  `Optional`\[`int`]\_[¶](#genai.types.GoogleTypeDateDict.year "Link to this definition")

Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without a year.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*pydantic model* genai.types.GroundingMetadata[¶](#genai.types.GroundingMetadata "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Metadata returned to client when grounding is enabled.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

},

"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Score indicating how likely information from Google Search could help answer the prompt. The score is in the range `\[0, 1\]`, where 0 is the least likely and 1 is the most likely. This score is only populated when Google Search grounding and dynamic retrieval is enabled. It will be compared to the threshold to determine whether to trigger Google Search.",

}

Source content flagging uri for a place or review.

This is currently populated only for Google Maps grounding. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

},
{

* [`confidence_scores (list[float] | None)`](#genai.types.GroundingSupport.confidence_scores "genai.types.GroundingSupport.confidence_scores")

Validators:

*field* confidence\_scores\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`float`]]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'confidenceScores')*[¶](#genai.types.GroundingSupport.confidence_scores "Link to this definition")

Confidence score of the support references. Ranges from 0 to 1. 1 is the most confident. For Gemini 2.0 and before, this list must have the same size as the grounding\_chunk\_indices. For Gemini 2.5 and after, this list will be empty and should be ignored.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* grounding\_chunk\_indices\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`int`]]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'groundingChunkIndices')*[¶](#genai.types.GroundingSupport.grounding_chunk_indices "Link to this definition")

A list of indices (into ‘grounding\_chunk’) specifying the citations associated with the claim. For instance \[1,3,4] means that grounding\_chunk\[1], grounding\_chunk\[3], grounding\_chunk\[4] are the retrieved content attributed to the claim.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* segment\_:  `Optional`\[[`Segment`](#genai.types.Segment "genai.types.Segment")]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.GroundingSupport.segment "Link to this definition")

Segment of the content this support belongs to.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

Grounding support.

confidence\_scores\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`float`]]\_[¶](#genai.types.GroundingSupportDict.confidence_scores "Link to this definition")

Confidence score of the support references. Ranges from 0 to 1. 1 is the most confident. For Gemini 2.0 and before, this list must have the same size as the grounding\_chunk\_indices. For Gemini 2.5 and after, this list will be empty and should be ignored.

grounding\_chunk\_indices\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[`int`]]\_[¶](#genai.types.GroundingSupportDict.grounding_chunk_indices "Link to this definition")

A list of indices (into ‘grounding\_chunk’) specifying the citations associated with the claim. For instance \[1,3,4] means that grounding\_chunk\[1], grounding\_chunk\[3], grounding\_chunk\[4] are the retrieved content attributed to the claim.

segment\_:  `Optional`\[[`SegmentDict`](#genai.types.SegmentDict "genai.types.SegmentDict")]\_[¶](#genai.types.GroundingSupportDict.segment "Link to this definition")

Segment of the content this support belongs to.

*class* genai.types.HarmBlockMethod(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockMethod "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

Specify if the threshold is used for probability or severity score.

If not specified, the threshold is used for probability score. This enum is not supported in Gemini API.

HARM\_BLOCK\_METHOD\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'HARM\_BLOCK\_METHOD\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockMethod.HARM_BLOCK_METHOD_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

The harm block method is unspecified.

PROBABILITY *=  'PROBABILITY'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockMethod.PROBABILITY "Link to this definition")

The harm block method uses the probability score.

SEVERITY *=  'SEVERITY'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockMethod.SEVERITY "Link to this definition")

The harm block method uses both probability and severity scores.

*class* genai.types.HarmBlockThreshold(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockThreshold "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

The harm block threshold.

BLOCK\_LOW\_AND\_ABOVE *=  'BLOCK\_LOW\_AND\_ABOVE'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockThreshold.BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE "Link to this definition")

Block low threshold and above (i.e. block more).

BLOCK\_MEDIUM\_AND\_ABOVE *=  'BLOCK\_MEDIUM\_AND\_ABOVE'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockThreshold.BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE "Link to this definition")

Block medium threshold and above.

BLOCK\_NONE *=  'BLOCK\_NONE'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockThreshold.BLOCK_NONE "Link to this definition")

Block none.

BLOCK\_ONLY\_HIGH *=  'BLOCK\_ONLY\_HIGH'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockThreshold.BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH "Link to this definition")

Block only high threshold (i.e. block less).

HARM\_BLOCK\_THRESHOLD\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'HARM\_BLOCK\_THRESHOLD\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockThreshold.HARM_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

Unspecified harm block threshold.

OFF *=  'OFF'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmBlockThreshold.OFF "Link to this definition")

Turn off the safety filter.

*class* genai.types.HarmCategory(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

Harm category.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_CIVIC\_INTEGRITY *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_CIVIC\_INTEGRITY'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_CIVIC_INTEGRITY "Link to this definition")

Election filter is not longer supported. The harm category is civic integrity.

Type:

Deprecated

HARM\_CATEGORY\_DANGEROUS\_CONTENT *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_DANGEROUS\_CONTENT'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_DANGEROUS_CONTENT "Link to this definition")

The harm category is dangerous content.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_HARASSMENT *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_HARASSMENT'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_HARASSMENT "Link to this definition")

The harm category is harassment.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_HATE\_SPEECH *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_HATE\_SPEECH'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_HATE_SPEECH "Link to this definition")

The harm category is hate speech.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_DANGEROUS\_CONTENT *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_DANGEROUS\_CONTENT'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_IMAGE_DANGEROUS_CONTENT "Link to this definition")

The harm category is image dangerous content. This enum value is not supported in Gemini API.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_HARASSMENT *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_HARASSMENT'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_IMAGE_HARASSMENT "Link to this definition")

The harm category is image harassment. This enum value is not supported in Gemini API.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_HATE *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_HATE'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_IMAGE_HATE "Link to this definition")

The harm category is image hate. This enum value is not supported in Gemini API.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_SEXUALLY\_EXPLICIT *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_SEXUALLY\_EXPLICIT'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_IMAGE_SEXUALLY_EXPLICIT "Link to this definition")

The harm category is image sexually explicit content. This enum value is not supported in Gemini API.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_JAILBREAK *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_JAILBREAK'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_JAILBREAK "Link to this definition")

The harm category is for jailbreak prompts. This enum value is not supported in Gemini API.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_SEXUALLY\_EXPLICIT *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_SEXUALLY\_EXPLICIT'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_SEXUALLY_EXPLICIT "Link to this definition")

The harm category is sexually explicit content.

HARM\_CATEGORY\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'HARM\_CATEGORY\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

The harm category is unspecified.

*classmethod* from\_file(*\**, *location*, *mime\_type=None*)[¶](#genai.types.Image.from_file "Link to this definition")

Lazy-loads an image from a local file or Google Cloud Storage.

Return type:

[`Image`](#genai.types.Image "genai.types.Image")

Parameters:

* **location** – The local path or Google Cloud Storage URI from which to load the image.

* **mime\_type** – The MIME type of the image. If not provided, the MIME type will be automatically determined.

Returns:

A loaded image as an Image object.

model\_post\_init(*context*, */*)[¶](#genai.types.Image.model_post_init "Link to this definition")

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Return type:

`None`

Parameters:

* **self** – The BaseModel instance.

* **context** – The context.

save(*location*)[¶](#genai.types.Image.save "Link to this definition")

Saves the image to a file.

Return type:

`None`

Parameters:

**location** – Local path where to save the image.

show()[¶](#genai.types.Image.show "Link to this definition")

Shows the image.

This method only works in a notebook environment.

`None`

*pydantic model* genai.types.InlinedRequest[¶](#genai.types.InlinedRequest "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Config for inlined request.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

"description":  "Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty \`response\` with \`will\_continue=False\` to signal that the function call is finished.",

*pydantic model* genai.types.InlinedResponse[¶](#genai.types.InlinedResponse "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Config for inlined\_responses parameter.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"additionalProperties":  true,
"type":  "object"
},
{

},
"willContinue":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

}
},

"FunctionResponse":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "A function response.",
"properties":  {
"willContinue":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON\_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty \`response\` with \`will\_continue=False\` to signal that the function call is finished.",
"title":  "Willcontinue"
},
"scheduling":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/FunctionResponseScheduling"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {

},
{

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},
"name":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Required. The name of the function to call. Matches \[FunctionDeclaration.name] and \[FunctionCall.name].",
"title":  "Name"
},
"response":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"additionalProperties":  true,
"type":  "object"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Required. The function response in JSON object format. Use \\"output\\" key to specify function output and \\"error\\" key to specify error details (if any). If \\"output\\" and \\"error\\" keys are not specified, then whole \\"response\\" is treated as function output.",
"title":  "Response"
}
},
"title":  "FunctionResponse",
"type":  "object"
},

"parsed":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/BaseModel"
},
{
"additionalProperties":  true,
"type":  "object"
},
{
"description":  "Create a collection of name/value pairs.\n\nExample enumeration:\n\n>>> class Color(Enum):\n...     RED = 1\n...     BLUE = 2\n...     GREEN = 3\n\nAccess them by:\n\n- attribute access:\n\n  >>> Color.RED\n  \<Color.RED: 1>\n\n- value lookup:\n\n  >>> Color(1)\n  \<Color.RED: 1>\n\n- name lookup:\n\n  >>> Color\['RED']\n  \<Color.RED: 1>\n\nEnumerations can be iterated over, and know how many members they have:\n\n>>> len(Color)\n3\n\n>>> list(Color)\n\[\<Color.RED: 1>, \<Color.BLUE: 2>, \<Color.GREEN: 3>]\n\nMethods can be added to enumerations, and members can have their own\nattributes -- see the documentation for details.",
"enum":  \[],
"title":  "Enum"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "First candidate from the parsed response if response\_schema is provided. Not available for streaming.",
"title":  "Parsed"
}
},
"title":  "GenerateContentResponse",
"type":  "object"
},

},
{

}
],
"default":  null,

},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The total number of tokens in the generated candidates.",
"title":  "Candidatestokencount"
},

"anyOf":  \[
{

},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the generated candidates.",
"title":  "Candidatestokensdetails"
},
"promptTokenCount":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},

}

"default":  null,

"GoogleTypeDate":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday.\n\nThe time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are\ninsignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can\nrepresent one of the following: \* A full date, with non-zero year, month, and\nday values. \* A month and day, with a zero year (for example, an anniversary).\n\* A year on its own, with a zero month and a zero day. \* A year and month,\nwith a zero day (for example, a credit card expiration date). Related types: \*\ngoogle.type.TimeOfDay \* google.type.DateTime \* google.protobuf.Timestamp. This\ndata type is not supported in Gemini API.",
"properties":  {
"day":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0 to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn't significant.",
"title":  "Day"
},
"month":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a month and day.",
"title":  "Month"
},
"year":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without a year.",
"title":  "Year"
}
},
"title":  "GoogleTypeDate",
"type":  "object"
},

"properties":  {
"displayName":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"

"GroundingSupport":  {

"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"type":  "number"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Confidence score of the support references. Ranges from 0 to 1. 1 is the most confident. For Gemini 2.0 and before, this list must have the same size as the grounding\_chunk\_indices. For Gemini 2.5 and after, this list will be empty and should be ignored.",
"title":  "Confidencescores"
},
"groundingChunkIndices":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"items":  {
"type":  "integer"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "A list of indices (into 'grounding\_chunk') specifying the citations associated with the claim. For instance \[1,3,4] means that grounding\_chunk\[1], grounding\_chunk\[3], grounding\_chunk\[4] are the retrieved content attributed to the claim.",
"title":  "Groundingchunkindices"
},
"segment":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Segment"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],

}
},
"title":  "GroundingSupport",
"type":  "object"
},
"HarmBlockThreshold":  {
"description":  "The harm block threshold.",
"enum":  \[

"BLOCK\_LOW\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_MEDIUM\_AND\_ABOVE",
"BLOCK\_ONLY\_HIGH",
"BLOCK\_NONE",
"OFF"
],
"title":  "HarmBlockThreshold",
"type":  "string"
},
"HarmCategory":  {
"description":  "Harm category.",
"enum":  \[

"HARM\_CATEGORY\_HARASSMENT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_HATE\_SPEECH",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_SEXUALLY\_EXPLICIT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_DANGEROUS\_CONTENT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_CIVIC\_INTEGRITY",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_HATE",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_DANGEROUS\_CONTENT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_HARASSMENT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_IMAGE\_SEXUALLY\_EXPLICIT",
"HARM\_CATEGORY\_JAILBREAK"
],
"title":  "HarmCategory",
"type":  "string"
},
"HarmProbability":  {

"NEGLIGIBLE",
"LOW",
"MEDIUM",
"HIGH"
],
"title":  "HarmProbability",
"type":  "string"
},
"HarmSeverity":  {
"description":  "Output only.\n\nHarm severity levels in the content. This enum is not supported in Gemini API.",
"enum":  \[
"HARM\_SEVERITY\_UNSPECIFIED",
"HARM\_SEVERITY\_NEGLIGIBLE",
"HARM\_SEVERITY\_LOW",
"HARM\_SEVERITY\_MEDIUM",
"HARM\_SEVERITY\_HIGH"
],
"title":  "HarmSeverity",
"type":  "string"
},

"Part":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,
"description":  "A datatype containing media content.\n\nExactly one field within a Part should be set, representing the specific type\nof content being conveyed. Using multiple fields within the same \`Part\`\ninstance is considered invalid.",
"properties":  {
"mediaResolution":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/PartMediaResolution"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Media resolution for the input media.\n    "
},
"codeExecutionResult":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/CodeExecutionResult"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Result of executing the \[ExecutableCode]."
},
"executableCode":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ExecutableCode"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Code generated by the model that is meant to be executed."
},
"fileData":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/FileData"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. URI based data."
},
"functionCall":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/FunctionCall"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. A predicted \[FunctionCall] returned from the model that contains a string representing the \[FunctionDeclaration.name] with the parameters and their values."
},
"functionResponse":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/FunctionResponse"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. The result output of a \[FunctionCall] that contains a string representing the \[FunctionDeclaration.name] and a structured JSON object containing any output from the function call. It is used as context to the model."
},
"inlineData":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/Blob"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Inlined bytes data."
},
"text":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Text part (can be code).",
"title":  "Text"
},
"thought":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "boolean"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Indicates if the part is thought from the model.",
"title":  "Thought"
},
"thoughtSignature":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"format":  "base64url",
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

"title":  "Thoughtsignature"
},
"videoMetadata":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/VideoMetadata"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Video metadata. The metadata should only be specified while the video data is presented in inline\_data or file\_data."
}
},
"title":  "Part",
"type":  "object"
},

*pydantic model* genai.types.Interval[¶](#genai.types.Interval "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a Timestamp end (exclusive).

The start must be less than or equal to the end. When the start equals the end, the interval is empty (matches no time). When both start and end are unspecified, the interval matches any time.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "Interval",
"description":  "Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a Timestamp end (exclusive).\n\nThe start must be less than or equal to the end. When the start equals the\nend, the interval is empty (matches no time). When both start and end are\nunspecified, the interval matches any time.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"endTime":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"format":  "date-time",
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Exclusive end of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the end.",
"title":  "Endtime"
},
"startTime":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"format":  "date-time",
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Inclusive start of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same or after the start.",
"title":  "Starttime"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

Fields:

* [`end_time (datetime.datetime | None)`](#genai.types.Interval.end_time "genai.types.Interval.end_time")

* [`start_time (datetime.datetime | None)`](#genai.types.Interval.start_time "genai.types.Interval.start_time")

Validators:

*field* end\_time\_:  `Optional`\[`datetime`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'endTime')*[¶](#genai.types.Interval.end_time "Link to this definition")

Optional. Exclusive end of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the end.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* start\_time\_:  `Optional`\[`datetime`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'startTime')*[¶](#genai.types.Interval.start_time "Link to this definition")

Optional. Inclusive start of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same or after the start.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*class* genai.types.IntervalDict[¶](#genai.types.IntervalDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a Timestamp end (exclusive).

The start must be less than or equal to the end. When the start equals the end, the interval is empty (matches no time). When both start and end are unspecified, the interval matches any time.

end\_time\_:  `Optional`\[`datetime`]\_[¶](#genai.types.IntervalDict.end_time "Link to this definition")

Optional. Exclusive end of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the end.

start\_time\_:  `Optional`\[`datetime`]\_[¶](#genai.types.IntervalDict.start_time "Link to this definition")

Optional. Inclusive start of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same or after the start.

*pydantic model* genai.types.JSONSchema[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchema "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

A subset of JSON Schema according to 2020-12 JSON Schema draft.

Represents a subset of a JSON Schema object that is used by the Gemini model. The difference between this class and the Schema class is that this class is compatible with OpenAPI 3.1 schema objects. And the Schema class is used to make API call to Gemini model.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

*class* genai.types.JSONSchemaType(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchemaType "Link to this definition")

Bases: `Enum`

The type of the data supported by JSON Schema.

The values of the enums are lower case strings, while the values of the enums for the Type class are upper case strings.

ARRAY *=  'array'*[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchemaType.ARRAY "Link to this definition")

BOOLEAN *=  'boolean'*[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchemaType.BOOLEAN "Link to this definition")

INTEGER *=  'integer'*[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchemaType.INTEGER "Link to this definition")

NULL *=  'null'*[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchemaType.NULL "Link to this definition")

NUMBER *=  'number'*[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchemaType.NUMBER "Link to this definition")

OBJECT *=  'object'*[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchemaType.OBJECT "Link to this definition")

STRING *=  'string'*[¶](#genai.types.JSONSchemaType.STRING "Link to this definition")

*pydantic model* genai.types.JobError[¶](#genai.types.JobError "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Job error.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "JobError",
"description":  "Job error.",
"type":  "object",

"type":  "string"
},
"type":  "array"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.",

},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},
"message":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the \`details\` field.",
"title":  "Message"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

*class* genai.types.JobState(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.JobState "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

Job state.

JOB\_STATE\_CANCELLED *=  'JOB\_STATE\_CANCELLED'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_CANCELLED "Link to this definition")

The job has been cancelled.

JOB\_STATE\_CANCELLING *=  'JOB\_STATE\_CANCELLING'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_CANCELLING "Link to this definition")

The job is being cancelled. From this state the job may only go to either JOB\_STATE\_SUCCEEDED, JOB\_STATE\_FAILED or JOB\_STATE\_CANCELLED.

JOB\_STATE\_EXPIRED *=  'JOB\_STATE\_EXPIRED'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_EXPIRED "Link to this definition")

The job has expired.

JOB\_STATE\_FAILED *=  'JOB\_STATE\_FAILED'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_FAILED "Link to this definition")

The job failed.

JOB\_STATE\_PARTIALLY\_SUCCEEDED *=  'JOB\_STATE\_PARTIALLY\_SUCCEEDED'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_PARTIALLY_SUCCEEDED "Link to this definition")

The job is partially succeeded, some results may be missing due to errors.

JOB\_STATE\_PAUSED *=  'JOB\_STATE\_PAUSED'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_PAUSED "Link to this definition")

The job has been stopped, and can be resumed.

JOB\_STATE\_PENDING *=  'JOB\_STATE\_PENDING'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_PENDING "Link to this definition")

The service is preparing to run the job.

JOB\_STATE\_QUEUED *=  'JOB\_STATE\_QUEUED'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_QUEUED "Link to this definition")

The job has been just created or resumed and processing has not yet begun.

JOB\_STATE\_RUNNING *=  'JOB\_STATE\_RUNNING'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_RUNNING "Link to this definition")

The job is in progress.

JOB\_STATE\_SUCCEEDED *=  'JOB\_STATE\_SUCCEEDED'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_SUCCEEDED "Link to this definition")

The job completed successfully.

JOB\_STATE\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'JOB\_STATE\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

The job state is unspecified.

JOB\_STATE\_UPDATING *=  'JOB\_STATE\_UPDATING'*[¶](#genai.types.JobState.JOB_STATE_UPDATING "Link to this definition")

The job is being updated. Only jobs in the JOB\_STATE\_RUNNING state can be updated. After updating, the job goes back to the JOB\_STATE\_RUNNING state.

*class* genai.types.Language(*\*values*)[¶](#genai.types.Language "Link to this definition")

Bases: `CaseInSensitiveEnum`

Programming language of the code.

LANGUAGE\_UNSPECIFIED *=  'LANGUAGE\_UNSPECIFIED'*[¶](#genai.types.Language.LANGUAGE_UNSPECIFIED "Link to this definition")

Unspecified language. This value should not be used.

PYTHON *=  'PYTHON'*[¶](#genai.types.Language.PYTHON "Link to this definition")

Python >= 3.10, with numpy and simpy available.

*pydantic model* genai.types.LatLng[¶](#genai.types.LatLng "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

An object that represents a latitude/longitude pair.

This is expressed as a pair of doubles to represent degrees latitude and degrees longitude. Unless specified otherwise, this object must conform to the \<a href=”<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Geodetic_System#1984_version>”> WGS84 standard\</a>. Values must be within normalized ranges.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Show JSON schema

{
"title":  "LatLng",
"description":  "An object that represents a latitude/longitude pair.\n\nThis is expressed as a pair of doubles to represent degrees latitude and\ndegrees longitude. Unless specified otherwise, this object must conform to the\n\<a href=\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World\_Geodetic\_System#1984\_version\\">\nWGS84 standard\</a>. Values must be within normalized ranges.",
"type":  "object",
"properties":  {
"latitude":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The latitude in degrees. It must be in the range \[-90.0, +90.0].",
"title":  "Latitude"
},
"longitude":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "number"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The longitude in degrees. It must be in the range \[-180.0, +180.0]",
"title":  "Longitude"
}
},
"additionalProperties":  false
}

* [`latitude (float | None)`](#genai.types.LatLng.latitude "genai.types.LatLng.latitude")

* [`longitude (float | None)`](#genai.types.LatLng.longitude "genai.types.LatLng.longitude")

Validators:

*field* latitude\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.LatLng.latitude "Link to this definition")

The latitude in degrees. It must be in the range \[-90.0, +90.0].

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* longitude\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.LatLng.longitude "Link to this definition")

The longitude in degrees. It must be in the range \[-180.0, +180.0]

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

Bases: `TypedDict`

An object that represents a latitude/longitude pair.

This is expressed as a pair of doubles to represent degrees latitude and degrees longitude. Unless specified otherwise, this object must conform to the \<a href=”<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Geodetic_System#1984_version>”> WGS84 standard\</a>. Values must be within normalized ranges.

latitude\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.LatLngDict.latitude "Link to this definition")

The latitude in degrees. It must be in the range \[-90.0, +90.0].

longitude\_:  `Optional`\[`float`]\_[¶](#genai.types.LatLngDict.longitude "Link to this definition")

The longitude in degrees. It must be in the range \[-180.0, +180.0]

"description":  "Optional. Specifies the nucleus sampling threshold. The model\n      considers only the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability is\n      at least \`top\_p\`. This helps generate more diverse and less repetitive\n      responses. For example, a \`top\_p\` of 0.9 means the model considers tokens\n      until the cumulative probability of the tokens to select from reaches 0.9.\n      It's recommended to adjust either temperature or \`top\_p\`, but not both.",
"title":  "Topp"

"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "integer"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "Optional. Specifies the top-k sampling threshold. The model\n      considers only the top k most probable tokens for the next token. This can\n      be useful for generating more coherent and less random text. For example,\n      a \`top\_k\` of 40 means the model will choose the next word from the 40 most\n      likely words.",
"title":  "Topk"

*pydantic model* genai.types.LiveClientContent[¶](#genai.types.LiveClientContent "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Incremental update of the current conversation delivered from the client.

All the content here will unconditionally be appended to the conversation history and used as part of the prompt to the model to generate content.

A message here will interrupt any current model generation.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

Fields:

* [`turn_complete (bool | None)`](#genai.types.LiveClientContent.turn_complete "genai.types.LiveClientContent.turn_complete")

* [`turns (list[genai.types.Content] | None)`](#genai.types.LiveClientContent.turns "genai.types.LiveClientContent.turns")

Validators:

*field* turn\_complete\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_ *=  None* *(alias 'turnComplete')*[¶](#genai.types.LiveClientContent.turn_complete "Link to this definition")

If true, indicates that the server content generation should start with the currently accumulated prompt. Otherwise, the server will await additional messages before starting generation.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*field* turns\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`Content`](#genai.types.Content "genai.types.Content")]]\_ *=  None*[¶](#genai.types.LiveClientContent.turns "Link to this definition")

The content appended to the current conversation with the model.

For single-turn queries, this is a single instance. For multi-turn queries, this is a repeated field that contains conversation history and latest request.

Validated by:

* `_check_field_type_mismatches`

*class* genai.types.LiveClientContentDict[¶](#genai.types.LiveClientContentDict "Link to this definition")

Bases: `TypedDict`

Incremental update of the current conversation delivered from the client.

All the content here will unconditionally be appended to the conversation history and used as part of the prompt to the model to generate content.

A message here will interrupt any current model generation.

turn\_complete\_:  `Optional`\[`bool`]\_[¶](#genai.types.LiveClientContentDict.turn_complete "Link to this definition")

If true, indicates that the server content generation should start with the currently accumulated prompt. Otherwise, the server will await additional messages before starting generation.

turns\_:  `Optional`\[`list`\[[`ContentDict`](#genai.types.ContentDict "genai.types.ContentDict")]]\_[¶](#genai.types.LiveClientContentDict.turns "Link to this definition")

The content appended to the current conversation with the model.

For single-turn queries, this is a single instance. For multi-turn queries, this is a repeated field that contains conversation history and latest request.

*pydantic model* genai.types.LiveClientMessage[¶](#genai.types.LiveClientMessage "Link to this definition")

Bases: `BaseModel`

Messages sent by the client in the API call.

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises \[ValidationError]\[pydantic\_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

"anyOf":  \[
{

},
{
"type":  "null"
}

},
"title":  "ExecutableCode",
"type":  "object"
},
"ExternalApi":  {
"additionalProperties":  false,

"apiAuth":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ApiAuth"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],

},
"apiSpec":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ApiSpec"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The API spec that the external API implements."
},
"authConfig":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/AuthConfig"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,
"description":  "The authentication config to access the API."
},

"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ExternalApiElasticSearchParams"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},
"endpoint":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"type":  "string"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

},
"simpleSearchParams":  {
"anyOf":  \[
{
"$ref":  "#/$defs/ExternalApiSimpleSearchParams"
},
{
"type":  "null"
}
],
"default":  null,

}
},
"title":  "ExternalApi",
"type":  "object"
},

"index":  {

{

},
{

}
],
"default":  null,

},
{

}
],
"default":  null,

},

{

}
],

},

"enum":  \[
"FEATURE\_SELECTION\_PREFERENCE\_UNSPECIFIED",

],
"title":  "FeatureSelectionPreference",
